subject: Science
chapter: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

key_points: The human eye is a natural optical instrument that helps us see objects
The eye forms a real and inverted image of objects on the retina
The eye lens can change its focal length to focus objects at different distances
The ability of the eye to adjust focus is called accommodation
Defects of vision include myopia, hypermetropia and presbyopia
White light splits into different colours when it passes through a prism
Scattering of light in the atmosphere causes many natural phenomena
examples: Myopia (short-sightedness) is corrected using a concave lens
Hypermetropia (long-sightedness) is corrected using a convex lens
Presbyopia occurs due to ageing of the eye lens
Stars appear to twinkle due to atmospheric refraction
The sky appears blue due to scattering of blue light by the atmosphere
The Sun appears reddish during sunrise and sunset
formulas: Near point of normal eye → 25 cm
Far point of normal eye → Infinity
Spectrum → Band of colours obtained when white light disperses
Dispersion → Splitting of white light into its component colours
Scattering → Deflection of light in different directions by particles in a medium
exam_tips: Remember the parts and functions of the human eye
Understand the causes and corrections of vision defects
Practice ray diagrams for myopia and hypermetropia
Learn the reasons for natural phenomena like blue sky and red sunset
Revise key terms such as dispersion, spectrum and scattering
